mnemonics.title: M-ary Bits Formula
mnemonics.content: M-ary: 'M-ary equals log base 2 of M' - remember: bits = log₂M, so M = 2^b

acronyms.usage: M-ary signaling uses M distinct signals
Determines error performance of signal set

acronyms.expansion: Energy per bit to Noise spectral density

visualTips.visualization: Draw two circles - orthogonal as perpendicular axes, antipodal as opposite points on a line
Imagine grid points - QPSK as square corners, BPSK as two opposite points on line
Think of 'as the crow flies' distance between signal points in 2D plane
M=2 is 2 points, M=4 is 4 points, M=8 is 8 points - more points crowded in same space

analogies.analogy: Like alphabet - 26 letters (signals) to form words (data)
Like perpendicular streets - no overlap or interference
Like spacing between letters on keyboard - more spacing = fewer typos
Like choosing words vs letters - longer words (higher M) transmit more info per unit time
